mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without overheating, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive GDP growth and stable CPI yields negative 60d forward returns in > 55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 50% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending, immediately reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where RSAFS drops > 1.0% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, supporting higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60d returns in > 50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, increases in the federal funds rate trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases during tightening cycles produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained upward trends in industrial production indicate expanding capacity utilization and stronger industrial demand, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO trends over a 3-year window.
